title: brassica oleracea subsp. acephala
intro: Brassica oleracea subsp. acephala, also known as kale, is a cool-season crop belonging to the Brassica family and is widely cultivated for its edible leaves. The leaves and stems of the kale plant possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which can aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Consuming kale has been linked to various health benefits, such as improving eye health, supporting bone health, and enhancing the immune system. In herbal preparations, kale is often used in the form of tea, capsules, or added to salads, providing a nutrient-dense addition to a balanced diet.
